post.content:
My profile reference:
College: Tier 2
Current Company: Well funded startup
Leetcode solved questions: 60
Leetcode seen questions: 140
Preparatio Time: Around 1 month
Exp: 2.5 yrs

Rounds:

**Round 1. CodeSignal Test (1 hour test)**
This was screening test. test had Leetcode Easy/Medium questions. Time was more than sufficient. I solved both questions in 30 minutes.

**Round 2. Machine Coding Round (1 hour)**
Problem was based on concurrency. Can't tell the exact problem, but its somewhat related to job scheduler. You need to explain your approach and code it in given time. Code should be demoable and should handle concurency and multithreadiing issues. My code was working with all edge cases passed. You can write code in your own IDE.

I would recommend to practice for this round as I feel this round is trickier than other rounds.

**Round 3: Problem Solving (1 hour)**
One coding question (Leetcode Hard) was asked. Problem was based on segment trees and I was not aware of the concept, so was not able to get to solution initilally, then got hints from interviewer and was able to write working code within time.
Note: Code is not cmpiled for this round and platform used was CodeSignal.

**Round 4: System Design(1 hour)**
I was asked to design a system to Facebook Messanger. Even though I was well prepared for this design, but faced few diffculties around interviwer's additional requirement and need to change schema a lot. Still I think, interviewer was satosfied with approach and design decision

**Round 5: Hiring Manager(1 hour)**
He asked all abput my current projects , its design , my role in that project along with some situational based questions. I was well prepared for these rounds, so was able to nailed it easily.

**Round 6: Bar Raiser (1 hour)**
This round as also simmilar to HM round, but Interviewer was going very deep into my current projects, after a lot discussion regarding design decisions of my current projects , he was quite impressed. No seperate design/PSDS questions were asked.

**After 4 days , I was informed that its a Hire call and they would like to extend an offer.**

I also gave interview at Google but was offered L3: https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-experience/1228229/Google-or-L3L4-or-India-or-L3-Offer

I would be happy to answer, if you have any more questions. Please don't ask about offer details here

I'm seriously stunned to know that you did write segment tree working code without knowing the concept or having practiced before. 
Congratualtions anyway :)

What's the TC for Uber ?
Which resources helped you to solve concurrency, multithreading problems ?

@OP, I am in the middle of the UBER SDE-2 interview process, MC and PS rounds are done. In the MC , wasn\'t able to complete it but had almost written the logic(85-90 % completion) and for the PS , wrote the logic but upon running the  code , it wasn\'t giving the appropriate result even though the approach was in the right direction. <https://leetcode.com/problems/word-search-ii/> was asked this question, gave the DFS approach to it. Have my SD round after 3 days, could you tell me my odds of selection even if manage to ace the SD round ? Thnx in adv
For machine coding, did they allow you to use inbuit libraries like in java we have ExecutorService etc?
can I know which topics of ds and algo did you prepare for coding interviews???
LLD round was done on which platform bro please tell.\n\n

Few questions on the machine coding round
1. What sources did you use?
2. Implementation was done using a framework or vanilla Java?
3. Was it focused on handling concurrency or database and class designing?

What was the background verification procedure there? My brother has received an offer from Uber recently and would like to know if they conduct employment background checks through any third party and to what extent?  He does not have a formal experience letter from the first organization.
@OP, in how many days did u get the confirmation after the System Design round?

Can you please tell me the approx timeline and schedule of the interviews.\n1. How many rounds happened in a day ?\n2. How much time it took approximately after attempting the online test to get the onsite done and rolling out offer
Do you have competitive programming exp?\nCracking Uber with one month of preparation is amazing.\nDid you solve problems in any other platform or just leetcode?
Any Tips you wanted to give us regarding HM and Bar Raiser round, I recently f****kd up this part in my SWE intern interview. I was unable to explain it well, what tips you can give, pls share.
Congrats :)\nPlease can you tell the strategy to get that big number from Uber without counter offers near that number?
Hey, What were the question asked in the CodeSignal Test? Would be helpful for the community :)\n
